cover.headline: Why alcohol destroys your deep sleep
cover.subtitle: The science behind why you wake at 3am—and when that changes

slides.title: What alcohol does at night
items: REM sleep suppressed, so your brain skips its restore cycle
Cortisol spikes early, pulling you into lighter sleep stages
Adenosine (your natural sleep drive) scrambled, causing fragmented rest

slides.title: The first two weeks sober
items: Sleep fragments at first, as your brain recalibrates without alcohol
Vivid dreams surge by day 7, marking REM returning at full force
By day 14, you're falling asleep faster without the sedative effect

slides.title: By month one and beyond
items: Deep sleep (N3) lengthens, so your body finally repairs overnight
Cortisol normalizes, meaning fewer 3am wake-ups and racing thoughts
Memory consolidation improves as REM cycles complete uninterrupted

end.saveLine: Save this if you're struggling to sleep without alcohol
end.shareLine: Share with someone who drinks to wind down at night
end.closer: Your brain still knows how to sleep
